instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Already Approved Article Link: https://www.bajajallianzlife.com/life-insurance-guide/term/1-crore-term-insurance-plan.html
What Is 1 Crore Term Insurance
Term insurance plans have evolved significantly over the years, and today, they offer a number of features and benefits that prove to be extremely useful. You can now be rest assured that in case of your untimely or sudden demise, your family will have a substantial amount to fall back on.
Both offline and online term plans offer sum assured amounts as high as starting from 1 Crore Term Insurance Plan to up to ₹100 Crore and more also. Sum assured can be picked or selected by an individual depending on their requirements. With a plan like this, you can secure your future and the future of your family.
What Does Sum Assured Mean In Term Insurance?
In simple terms, the sum assured is the value of insurance cover provided by the insurer when you purchase the term plan. It’s the amount that the insurer pays out to the beneficiary in case of death of the life insured. For instance, say you purchase an online term plan where the insurer agrees to pay a sum of ₹1 crore in the event of your unfortunate demise. That amount is referred to as the sum assured.
So, let’s say when you purchase a ₹1 crore term insurance plan, you are eligible for a sum assured of ₹1 crore. Here are some other points to note about the sum assured under an insurance policy.
• The premium charged for a term insurance policy depends, among other things, on the sum assured chosen by you.
• You can make use of an online term insurance calculator to understand how the amount of premium you pay changes as the sum assured increases.
• The premium you pay can be deducted from your total taxable income as per section 80C, under old tax regime of the Income Tax Act, 1961. However, to be eligible for these tax benefits, you need to ensure that the sum assured is at least 10 times the annual premium charged for the policy.
• The sum assured, which is received as part of the death benefits under the insurance plan, is exempt from tax as per section 10(10D) of the Income Tax Act, 1961. The aforementioned term insurance tax benefits are subject to changes in tax laws, as amended from time to time.
Many online term plans as well as offline insurance policies offer sum assured amounts starting from ₹1 crore up to ₹100 Crore and even more. Opting for a high death benefit can be incredibly helpful to you and your family. Let’s see how.
How Does 1 Crore Term Insurance Plan Work?
A Rs.1 crore term insurance plan is like any other term plan with a high sum assured. When you buy the policy, you choose the following details –
Sum assured, which is Rs.1 crore
The policy term
The premium paying term
The premium paying frequency
Optional riders, if any
Based on these details, your age and other factors, the premium is determined. You pay the premium for the chosen tenure and in the chosen frequency.
During the term of the policy, if the life insured passes away, the term insurance policy pays the death benefit which is, usually, Rs.1 crore in case you opt for a Rs 1 crore term insurance plan. If, however, the policy matures and the life insured is alive, there is usually no maturity benefit. The coverage is simply terminated. This is, however, in the case of a pure term plan.
However, if you buy a Rs. 1 crore term insurance with a return of premium option, the premiums paid during the policy tenure are refunded if the plan matures and the life insured survives the tenure. However, in the event of unfortunate death within the policy tenure, the Rs. 1 crore term insurance will pay out the entire sum assured of Rs. 1 crore to the nominee as death benefit.
For instance, Bajaj Allianz Smart Protection Goal is a comprehensive term insurance plan that offers Rs.1 crore coverage. When buying the policy you choose the sum assured, the critical illness coverage or return of premium feature, policy term, premium paying term and frequency.
The plan runs for the chosen tenure. If you have chosen the critical illness cover, you get covered for 55 critical illnesses and if you choose the return of premium feature, the premiums are returned on maturity. You can get whole-life coverage too and the plan pays the death benefit on premature demise.
What Are The Benefits Of Term Plans With A Sum Assured Of ₹1 Crore?
A high sum assured comes with several upsides. By investing in a ₹1 crore term insurance plan, you stand to benefit in a number of ways. Here’s a closer look at the benefits of term plans with a sum assured amount of ₹1 crore.
A higher sum assured acts as a hedge against inflation
A quick look at the past shows us that prices have risen significantly over these years. The costs of basic amenities have increased. Medical care is more expensive, as are other facilities like education and housing. This trend is certain to continue into the future. So, if you wish to ensure that your family has sufficient funds to fall back on, you’ll find that a ₹1 crore term insurance plan is one of the preferred investment option. However, if you want to take a plan with a sum assured of more than ₹1 Crore, you can do that depending on your risk appetite and life goals. A smaller sum may not be enough to get your family through everyday life if the costs soar higher, as they’re bound to.
It helps your family meet significant expenses in the future
If you invest in a ₹1 crore term insurance plan in your twenties, your plan will probably expire only when you’re in your fifties or so. Some plans even offer longer coverage, so your insurance remains valid till you’re in your sixties. In case of your sudden demise, there could be situations where your family does not have enough savings, hence your family will have no way to pay for significant expenses in the future without a life cover. Costs like your children’s educational expenses, their wedding outlays, and any serious medical expenditure can be handled more smoothly if your family has the benefits of at least a ₹1 crore term insurance plan. They could have a term insurance plan with higher sum assured also.
How Do You Determine If A 1 Crore Term Insurance Plan Is Suitable For You?
To determine whether the term insurance plan is suitable for you, you need to factor in several factors. Some of these factors are as follows –
The policy term
Check whether the policy term aligns with your protection needs. It is better to choose a term which is high so that you can enjoy coverage for longer and into your old age.
Premium paying term and frequency
The premium paying term is the period over which you are required to pay the premium to enjoy complete coverage. Choose a term which is suitable and over which you will be able to pay the premium easily. For instance, say you choose coverage till 75 years of age but you expect to retire at 65. In this case, a limited premium paying term which ends after 65 years of age is better so that you don’t have to pay the premiums but you can enjoy the coverage till 75.
Similarly, choose a frequency over which you can pay the premium affordably. If the annual premium is tight on your budget, break it down into quarterly, half-yearly or even monthly premiums for affordable payments.
Sufficiency of the sum assured
Assess whether the sum assured is suitable for your financial needs. You can use life insurance calculators available online to find the estimated sum assured amount. You can also use the Human Life Value (HLV)1 calculation method or use the Thumb Rule, wherein the optimal sum assured is determined as 10 times your annual income2.
If Rs.1 crore sum assured is optimal, you can buy the coverage else you can opt for a higher or lower sum assured depending on your needs. However, remember to keep inflation mind as the coverage amount might rise with time.
The coverage offered
Lastly, check the coverage offered. Usually, term plans have a simple coverage structure but, in some cases, you might get additional protection through riders bought at an extra nominal cost. Assess the coverage available and ensure that it fulfils your protection needs.
Reasons For Considering 1 Crore Term Insurance
With a Rs.1 crore life insurance plan the family receives considerable financial assistance which can help them take care of their financial needs and avoid a possible strain, in the event of the insured person’s demise.
Moreover, when the payout is optimal, the family may also use the funds to meet their financial goals and live a financially independent life.
So, if the optimal coverage lies in the range of Rs.1 crore and you have a suitable income to avail of that coverage, opting for the Rs.1 crore term plan is a good choice as it provides the required financial protection. However, you need to keep your income and other factors in mind and then decide the coverage amount. In case you wish to enhance the coverage amount, you can always opt for the same or opt for multiple Rs. 1 crore term plans to match your suitability.
Who Should Opt For A 1 Crore Term Plan?
The suitability of the Rs.1 crore term insurance plan depends on various factors like your age, income, number of dependents, existing assets and liabilities, financial goals, etc. Individuals can use the term insurance calculator to find out the optimal sum assured based on their financial needs. If the suitability calculation calls for a Rs.1 crore term plan, they can buy the policy for financial protection.
The calculator is available online and helps understand the coverage needs. Alternatively, individuals can multiply their annual income by 102 to understand whether they need Rs.1 crore term insurance coverage or not.
The Human Life Value1 method can also point out individuals who can opt for the Rs.1 crore term insurance plan.
In short, every individual can assess the coverage need and then buy the Rs.1 crore term plan if the sum assured matches their needs.
What Are Some Factors To Keep In Mind While Considering Term Plans?
Before investing in an online term plan or an offline term policy, you need to consider certain factors. This strategy helps you make an informed and prudent choice. Here are some things to keep in mind before you purchase a term insurance plan.
The life cover offered
The life cover or the sum assured is one of the most important aspects to look into before you put your money on an insurance plan. Take your regular annual expenses into account, make room for inflation, and add a sizable margin to determine the life cover that’s ideal for you. If you have a family with 2 or more children, a ₹1 crore term insurance plan is what your family may ideally need to get through in case you’re no longer around. However, this would not be it. It is always a good idea to take a few factors into consideration like liability, age, how long one intends to work etc. before deciding on your sum assured. There are a lot of term insurance calculators available which could give you an idea of what your right insure amount should be depending on multiple factors.
The policy duration
Since term insurance plans are pure life covers, it’s advisable to opt for policies that come with a longer duration. This way, your life cover is valid for a longer period. Ideally, your plan must extend into your sixties or later, so you’re covered up to your retirement or post retirement age. Some term insurance plans also offer whole life coverage, so you’re protected till you’re 99 years of age.
The insurer’s claim settlement ratio
This is the ratio of the claims settled by an insurer to the total number of claims received by them. The higher the claim settlement ratio is, the better are the chances of your family’s claims being paid out by the insurer. It’s advisable to look into the claim settlement ratio of various insurance providers and choose a suitable insurer to purchase your term plan from. You can take a look at the IRDAI’s annual report to find these ratios.
The rider options available
Term insurance plans do not offer any maturity benefits. However, to enhance the cover offered, many insurers now provide rider options on term plans. Some such riders include critical illness benefit rider, accidental death benefit rider, accidental disability riders, and waiver of premium rider. Look for the rider options available before you purchase a term plan, so you can enjoy a wider range of benefits.
Conclusion
You can use an online term insurance calculator to determine your life insurance cover requirement. ₹1 Crore term insurance plan would not be the only solution as insurance providers provide sum assured which could be more than ₹100 Crore also. Hence one should always evaluate their needs before deciding on their life cover requirement. Adequate term insurance plans help in ensuring security of your loved ones.
FAQs
What are the eligibility criteria for buying a 1 crore term insurance?
The eligibility criteria depend on the plan that you choose to buy. There’s usually a minimum and maximum entry age for buying a term policy. Resident individuals as well as Non-Resident Indians (NRIs) can buy a Rs.1 crore term insurance plan, basis requirement.
Is choosing a higher coverage option preferable?
A higher coverage option is preferable if such coverage matches your financial needs. In such cases, the higher coverage gives your family a fund that can meet their financial needs despite rising inflation. It allows higher protection and can also help your family fulfil their financial goals even in your absence.
Sources:
https://www.livemint.com/Money/5qLRIiCEtTvy4aMRd3cNPO/Human-life-value-is-used-to-estimate-required-sum-insured.html
https://www.livemint.com/money/personal-finance/four-methods-to-calculate-how-much-term-insurance-you-need-11605023306997.html
Disclaimers:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
